macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Breaks Below 200-Day Moving Average Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.37% for the week and is down 6.7% from its all-time high, breaking below its 200-day moving average as Middle East conflict and energy shocks drive market volatility. The index remains headline-driven as investors assess potential duration of regional conflict.

items.title: Fed Signals Hawkish Stance with Limited Rate Cut Expectations
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's median dot plot indicates only one rate cut expected in 2026 and one in 2027, with Chair Powell noting insufficient progress on inflation. A hotter-than-expected February Core PPI reading of +0.5% m/m versus 0.3% consensus added to inflation concerns.

items.title: Major Indices Show Weakness; Dow Down 1.14% for the Week
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 1.14% for the week and 5.86% for March, while the Nasdaq fell 0.06% weekly and 2.49% for the month. The MSCI EAFE dropped 0.87% weekly and 9.37% for March, reflecting global market stress.

items.title: FTSE 100 Experiences Sharp 2.35% Decline in Technically Pivotal Session
items.summary: The FTSE 100 collapsed 2.35% on March 20, 2026, breaking through critical technical levels as global equity indices face significant headwinds.

items.title: Multiple Corporate Announcements and Index Changes
items.summary: Notable developments include Unusual Machines pricing a $150 million public offering, TRNR authorizing a $500,000 stock repurchase program, and Cardinal Infrastructure Group being added to the Russell 2000 and 3000 indexes.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady Amid Market Decoupling and Quadruple Witching Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin trades around $70,600 with modest gains (+0.35% to +1.15%), decoupling from traditional markets like S&P 500 and Gold amid ETF outflows and the high-risk quadruple witching event. Upcoming $13.5B crypto derivatives expiry on Deribit adds to turbulence.

items.title: FET and PI Network Surge on AI Momentum and Protocol Upgrades
items.summary: Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (FET) rises over 17% in 24h fueled by AI sector hype and OpenAI developments, while PI Network (PI) gains 25%+ after Protocol v23 upgrade with AI-enhanced KYC and mainnet migration.

items.title: MicroStrategy Closes Gap on BlackRock in Bitcoin Accumulation Race
items.summary: MicroStrategy (Strategy) acquired 40,332 BTC in the week to mid-March 2026, narrowing the holdings gap with BlackRock to ~23,000 coins and accelerating Bitcoin supply squeeze alongside ETFs absorbing over 2M BTC.

items.title: US Congress Schedules Tokenization Hearing Next Week
items.summary: US Congress will hold a hearing on tokenization next week, featuring testimony from the Blockchain Association CEO, amid growing regulatory focus on blockchain applications.

items.title: Pepeto Meme Coin Presale Nears Record $8.1M Raised
items.summary: Pepeto meme coin presale hits $8.1M at $0.000000186 per token, approaching confirmed exchange listings and topping crypto news for presale performance in March 2026.

items.title: Quant, Chainlink, and Stellar Trend on Listings and Launches
items.summary: Quant (QNT) tops trends after Robinhood listing, Chainlink faces community disputes, and Stellar advances with tokenized fund launch, signaling potential short-term corrections from hype.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Sells 2 Million Copies Overnight Despite Performance Issues
items.summary: Pearl Abyss' Crimson Desert, released on March 19, 2026, sold 2 million copies worldwide in less than a day and peaked at 250,000 concurrent Steam players, though console versions suffer from stuttering frame rates and optimization problems.[3][4][5]

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Becomes Best-Selling Game of 2026 So Far
items.summary: Capcom's Resident Evil Requiem has crossed 6 million sales worldwide, making it the fastest-selling Resident Evil title, top seller in February, and the best-selling game of 2026 overall according to Circana reports.[4]

items.title: Subnautica 2 Enters Early Access in May 2026 After Delays and Leadership Changes
items.summary: Krafton announced Subnautica 2 will launch in Early Access in May 2026 following controversies, director removals, and a court-ordered CEO reinstatement at Unknown Worlds Entertainment.[2]

items.title: NVIDIA Introduces DLSS 5 with AI-Driven Visuals; NCSoft Shifts to Player-Driven Development
items.summary: NVIDIA unveiled DLSS 5 emphasizing AI-enhanced visual quality, while NCSoft announced reduced pay-to-win elements and more player-led decisions in development.[1]

items.title: Build a Rocket Boy Cuts Ties with IOI Partners, Cancels Hitman-MindsEye Crossover
items.summary: Developer Build a Rocket Boy ended its publishing deal with IO Interactive's IOI Partners amid sabotage claims, canceling the Hitman and MindsEye crossover but taking sole publishing forward.[2]

items.title: Steam Spring Sale 2026 Launches with Multiple Game Releases This Week
items.summary: The Steam Spring Sale 2026 is underway alongside recent releases like Ghost Master: Resurrection on March 20 and upcoming titles including Ariana and the Elder Codex on March 24.[1][3]

items.title: Redesigned Nintendo Switch 2 with Replaceable Battery Reportedly in Production for Europe
items.summary: A new Nintendo Switch 2 model featuring a replaceable battery is reportedly entering production for the European market, with no confirmation on wider availability.[4]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Releases Critical Security Update for WebKit Vulnerability
items.summary: Apple issued its first 'background security improvements' to address a critical WebKit vulnerability and warned users to update devices immediately against sophisticated hacking tools.

items.title: Micron Technology Reports Exceptional Q2 Earnings Beat
items.summary: Micron Technology exceeded Q2 expectations with EPS of $12.20 versus $8.66 consensus and revenue of $23.86B versus $19.74B expected, marking a 196.4% increase.

items.title: Tesla Signs $4.3 Billion Battery Deal with LG
items.summary: Tesla finalized a $4.3 billion battery supply agreement with LG, bolstering its energy storage and vehicle production capabilities.

items.title: Meta Secures 10-Year Lease for Fifth Avenue Flagship Store
items.summary: Meta signed a 10-year lease for a permanent flagship retail location on Fifth Avenue in New York, expanding its physical retail presence.

items.title: Fire Breaks Out at Czech Drone Manufacturer's Facility
items.summary: An industrial facility of LPP Holding, linked to defense drone projects, was set on fire in Pardubice, Czech Republic, on March 20.

items.title: Google Signs 2.7 GW Clean Energy Deal for Data Centers
items.summary: Google partnered with DTE Energy for a 2.7 GW clean energy commitment in Michigan to support its data center infrastructure expansion.
